Project: numereng
Status: stable CLI/API boundaries; feature-slice internals

Purpose:
- Numerai workflow package (train, submit, experiments, HPO, ensemble, neutralize, store, cloud, viz monitoring).
- Public surfaces: CLI (`numereng`) and Python facade (`import numereng.api`).
- Runtime baseline: Python `3.12+`; use the repo-managed `.venv` via `uv sync --extra dev` and run commands from the repo root with `uv run numereng ...`.

Read order:
1) docs/llms.txt
2) docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
3) README.md

Hard rules:
- Dependency direction is strict: `config -> platform -> features -> api -> cli`.
- `platform/*` must not import `features/*`.
- CLI remains thin; business logic stays in `features/*` behind `api/*`.
- Public surface folders: `src/numereng/api/`, `src/numereng/cli/`.
- API/CLI source files must stay <= 500 LOC.
- API convention: public modules are `numereng.api`, `numereng.api.pipeline`, and `numereng.api.contracts`; underscore-prefixed modules under `src/numereng/api/` are internal implementation only.

Boundary contracts:
- CLI entrypoint: `numereng = "numereng.cli:main"`.
- Python facade: `import numereng.api as api_module`.
- Stable typed contracts: `src/numereng/api/contracts.py`.
- Boundary error: `numereng.platform.errors.PackageError`.
- CLI exit codes: `0` success/help, `1` runtime/boundary, `2` parse/usage.

Source map:
- `src/numereng/config/training/*`: strict JSON training contracts + loader.
- `src/numereng/config/hpo/*`: strict JSON HPO contracts + loader.
- `src/numereng/config/cloud/*`: checked-in cloud runtime defaults such as the SageMaker image catalog.
- `src/numereng/platform/*`: boundary errors + Numerai adapter + forum scraper tooling.
- `src/numereng/platform/docs_sync.py`: shallow upstream docs clone helper used by repo-local Numerai docs sync.
- `src/numereng/features/training/*`: deterministic run orchestration + artifacts; `full_history_refit` runs now persist fitted model artifacts under `artifacts/model/`.
- `src/numereng/features/models/custom_models/*`: tracked plugin model adapters; numereng routes `TabPFNRegressor` cache traffic into `<effective_store_root>/cache/tabpfn` unless `TABPFN_MODEL_CACHE_DIR` is already set.
- `src/numereng/features/training/_pipeline.py`: staged internal train workflow (`prepare -> load_data -> train_model -> finalize`); training uses the materialized loader only, always writes fold-time `post_fold` artifacts for CV runs, and applies policy-driven post-training scoring (`none|core|full|round_core|round_full`).
- `src/numereng/features/scoring/*`: canonical run-scoring service + metric engines; hot-path metric math is array-kernel based in `features/scoring/_fastops.py` and orchestrated from `metrics.py`.
- `src/numereng/features/submission/*`: run/file prediction resolution + upload.
- `src/numereng/features/serving/*`: production submission packaging + preflight inspection + artifact-backed live inference + config-backed local fallback + weighted live blending + Numerai model-upload pickle assembly + package-native validation scoring + diagnostics snapshot sync.
- `src/numereng/features/baseline/*`: shared benchmark baseline construction from persisted run predictions; canonical outputs live under `.numereng/datasets/baselines/<name>/` and can promote the active benchmark artifact.
- `src/numereng/features/feature_neutralization/*`: prediction neutralization with a vectorized least-squares core; numereng preserves its run/file artifact workflow, `era|global` modes, and optional rank output while matching `numerai-tools` intercept handling.
- `src/numereng/features/experiments/*`: experiment manifests + archive-aware path resolution + source-owned run-plan execution state + train linkage + promote/report + deferred round scoring.
- `src/numereng/features/agentic_research/*`: minimal config-research loop. `PROGRAM.md` is the LLM policy; `run.py` is the deterministic runner. The LLM reads experiment configs, reports, notes, recent ledger rows, and research memory, then returns one JSON decision. Python validates allowed config paths, materializes a child config, trains, scores, and records artifacts under `.numereng/experiments/<id>/agentic_research/`. Planner compute source is selected from `src/numereng/config/openrouter/active-model.py` via `ACTIVE_MODEL_SOURCE` (`codex-exec|openrouter`); the checked-in default is `codex-exec`.
- `src/numereng/features/hpo/*`: Optuna orchestration + trial/study persistence.
- `src/numereng/features/ensemble/*`: rank-average build plus experiment-aware ensemble selection (candidate freeze from all available runs per target by default, pruning, equal-weight screening, exact weighted sweep) and persistence.
- `src/numereng/features/store/*`: sqlite schema/index/doctor/rebuild/layout.
- `src/numereng/features/docs_sync.py`: explicit repo-local Numerai docs mirror sync into `docs/numerai/`.
- `src/numereng/features/telemetry/*`: local run telemetry write path.
- `src/numereng/features/dataset-tools/*`: local dataset downsampling utilities for stored derived downsampled artifacts.
- `src/numereng/features/cloud/aws/*`: EC2 + managed AWS train/image/state.
- `src/numereng/features/cloud/modal/*`: Modal deploy/data/train/state.
- `viz/api/numereng_viz/*`: authoritative read-only viz backend package (routes, services, monitor snapshot composition, store merge logic).
- `src/numereng/features/viz/*`: compatibility shim only; no real viz business logic should live here.
- `src/numereng/platform/remotes/*`: tracked SSH remote-target schema/loader/docs + shell-aware SSH helpers; real user profiles live in the gitignored `profiles/` subdir or an override via `NUMERENG_REMOTE_PROFILES_DIR`. SSH targets declare `shell: posix|powershell`; use `powershell` for Windows remotes. Remote targets also declare `python_cmd` for helper scripts and may declare `runner_cmd` for detached CLI launches; Windows detached targets should point directly at the remote venv executables rather than `uv run ...`.
- `src/numereng/features/remote_ops/*`: SSH remote doctor + repo sync + runtime-owned experiment sync/pullback + ad hoc config push + detached remote train/experiment launch + maintain/stop/status helpers.
- `src/numereng/api/__init__.py`: curated public Python facade; research exports lazy-load so unrelated CLI/API entrypoints do not import `agentic_research` at package import time.
- `src/numereng/api/pipeline.py`: public workflow entrypoint for full local train flows.
- `src/numereng/api/_*.py`: internal API operation/stage wrappers; not stable import paths.
- `src/numereng/cli/*`: parsing/dispatch/help.

Workspace layout:
- Source-tree extension roots: `src/numereng/features/models/custom_models`, `src/numereng/features/agentic_research/PROGRAM.md`.
- Repo-local custom skill root: `.agents/skills` (local-only and gitignored).
- Optional repo-root synced vendor docs: `docs/numerai/` (downloaded manually via `numereng docs sync numerai`).
- Repo-managed runtime files: `pyproject.toml`, `.python-version`, `.venv/`.
- Hidden runtime store: `.numereng` (`numereng.db`, `runs`, `datasets`, `experiments`, `notes`, `cloud`, `cache`, `tmp`, `remote_ops`).
- Numereng-managed dirs under `.numereng`: `runs`, `datasets`, `experiments`, `notes`, `cloud`, `cache`, `tmp`, `remote_ops`.
- Top-level sqlite files under `.numereng`: `numereng.db`, `numereng.db-shm`, `numereng.db-wal`.
- Experiment-local dynamic roots may appear under `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/`: `hpo/`, `ensembles/`.
- Global dynamic runtime roots may also appear under `.numereng/`: `hpo/`, `ensembles/`.
- Legacy compatibility state may still appear under `cloud/` during migration, but new cloud writers use `cache/cloud/`.
- Canonical rolling research memory now lives under `.numereng/notes/__RESEARCH_MEMORY__/`:
  - `CURRENT.md` is the single rolling frontier / next-step note
  - `experiments/<experiment_id>.md` stores one durable review per completed experiment
  - `topics/*.md` stores hybrid topic ledgers (current synthesis + evidence log)
  - `decisions/*.md` is used only for material frontier shifts
  - `legacy-progression/` preserves the old `__PROGRESSION__` month summaries as historical inputs only

Key artifacts:
- `runs/<run_id>/run.json`
  - includes durable `execution` provenance for `local|remote_host|cloud`
- `runs/<run_id>/runtime.json`
- `runs/<run_id>/run.log`
- `runs/<run_id>/resolved.json`
- `runs/<run_id>/results.json`
- `runs/<run_id>/metrics.json`
- `runs/<run_id>/score_provenance.json`
- `runs/<run_id>/artifacts/predictions/*.parquet`
  - numereng-managed parquet artifacts use `ZSTD` compression level `3`
- `cache/cloud/<provider>/runs/<run_id>/state.json`
- `cache/cloud/<provider>/runs/<run_id>/pull/`
- `cache/cloud/<provider>/ops/<op_id>/state.json`
- `cache/remote_ops/pulls/<target_id>/...`
  - legacy read-only compatibility for older remote pullbacks; new `remote experiment pull` runs materialize full finished runs into `runs/<run_id>/`
- `tmp/remote-configs/*.json`
  - retention-managed staging for detached/ad hoc remote launches only; not canonical run provenance
- `remote_ops/experiment_run_plan/<experiment_id>__<start>_<end>.json`
  - durable source-owned run-plan state for local or remote experiment windows
- `tmp/lifecycle_smoke/<session>/*.json`
  - generated smoke-test configs; successful sessions delete their own session dir, failed sessions stay for debugging
- `datasets/baselines/<baseline_name>/baseline.json`
- `datasets/baselines/<baseline_name>/pred_<baseline_name>.parquet`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/experiment.json`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/EXPERIMENT.md`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/EXPERIMENT.pack.md`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/configs/*.json`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/submission_packages/<package_id>/package.json`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/submission_packages/<package_id>/artifacts/live/*`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/submission_packages/<package_id>/artifacts/pickle/model.pkl`
- `.numereng/runs/<run_id>/artifacts/model/model.pkl`
- `.numereng/runs/<run_id>/artifacts/model/manifest.json`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/run_scripts/*`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/agentic_research/state.json`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/agentic_research/ledger.jsonl`
- `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/agentic_research/rounds/rN/*`
  - canonical round artifacts are `decision.json`, `notes.md`, and `context.json` for LLM rounds; `debug/*` is written only on LLM/Codex failure
- `.numereng/experiments/_archive/<experiment_id>/...`

Wiring map:
- Bootstrap:
  `numereng [--fail] -> cli.main -> api.run_bootstrap_check`
- Repo bootstrap:
  `uv sync --extra dev`
- Numerai docs sync:
  `cli docs sync numerai -> api.sync_docs -> features.docs_sync.sync_numerai_docs -> platform.docs_sync.clone_shallow`
- Train:
  `cli run train -> bind cli metadata -> api.run_training -> api.run_training_pipeline (compat) -> features.training.run_training`
- Public workflow entrypoint:
  `numereng.api.pipeline.run_training_pipeline(TrainRunRequest) -> internal API stages -> features.training._pipeline`
- Re-score persisted run:
  `cli run score [--stage <canonical-stage>] -> bind cli metadata -> api.score_run -> features.scoring.run_service.score_run`
- Build shared benchmark baseline:
  `cli baseline build --run-ids <id1,id2,...> --name <baseline_name> [--default-target <target_col>] [--promote-active] -> api.baseline_build -> features.baseline.build_baseline`
- Request cooperative cancel:
  `cli run cancel --run-id <id> -> api.cancel_run -> features.telemetry.request_run_cancel`
- Experiment train:
  `cli experiment train -> bind cli metadata -> api.experiment_train -> features.experiments.train_experiment -> features.training.run_training`
- Experiment run-plan:
  `cli experiment run-plan -> api.experiment_run_plan -> features.experiments.run_experiment_plan`
- Experiment deferred round scoring:
  `cli experiment score-round -> bind cli metadata -> api.experiment_score_round -> features.experiments.score_experiment_round -> features.scoring.batch_service.score_run_batch`
- Experiment pack:
  `cli experiment pack -> api.experiment_pack -> features.experiments.pack_experiment`
- Agentic research:
  `cli research status|run -> api.research_status|research_run -> features.agentic_research.get_research_status|run_research`
  - `research run` initializes state on first use; there is no program catalog or init command
  - planner backend is selected at runtime from `src/numereng/config/openrouter/active-model.py` (`ACTIVE_MODEL_SOURCE`); checked-in default is `codex-exec`
  - `state.json` and `ledger.jsonl` persist lightweight state and round history
  - when `ACTIVE_MODEL_SOURCE=codex-exec`, planner runtime uses the user’s ambient Codex config and environment; agentic research does not create a separate learner `CODEX_HOME`
  - the mutable surface is experiment config JSON; the LLM is not allowed to edit Python source
  - the planner returns one JSON object with `action`, learning fields, `parent_config`, and config changes; Python clones the parent config, applies the changes, validates the new `TrainingConfig`, detects duplicates, and writes one child config
- Experiment archive:
  `cli experiment archive|unarchive -> api.experiment_archive|experiment_unarchive -> features.experiments.archive_experiment|unarchive_experiment -> move experiment dir + update manifest + reindex experiment row`
- Submit:
  `cli run submit -> api.submit_predictions -> features.submission`
- Serve:
  `cli serve package create|inspect|list|score|sync-diagnostics -> api.serve_package_* -> features.serving`
  `cli serve live build|submit -> api.serve_live_* -> features.serving -> features.submission`
  `cli serve pickle build|upload -> api.serve_pickle_* -> features.serving -> features.submission`
  - hosted model uploads are stricter than local live builds: `pickle_upload_ready` only becomes true after an isolated hosted-runtime smoke succeeds for the selected Numerai docker image, and exported pickles must be self-contained rather than depending on local `numereng` imports
  - `serve package score` evaluates the final package artifact on local validation data, persists package-native artifacts under `submission_packages/<package_id>/artifacts/eval/validation/<runtime>/`, and reuses the generic scoring kernel without creating a synthetic run
- `serve package sync-diagnostics` persists upload-scoped compute status/logs plus the latest available Numerai model diagnostics snapshot under `submission_packages/<package_id>/artifacts/diagnostics/<upload_id>/`
  - `serve pickle upload --wait-diagnostics` uploads first, then waits for/persists diagnostics without changing the package deployment status
- HPO:
  `cli hpo create -> api.hpo_create -> features.hpo.create_study -> validate v2 study spec + resume by study_id -> JournalStorage-backed Optuna -> per-trial run_training + index_run + study summary/trial parquet updates`
- Ensemble:
  `cli ensemble build -> api.ensemble_build -> features.ensemble.build_ensemble`
  `cli ensemble select -> api.ensemble_select -> features.ensemble.select_ensemble`
- Neutralize:
  `cli neutralize apply -> api.neutralize_apply -> features.feature_neutralization`
- Store:
  `cli store * -> api.store_* -> features.store`
  - `cli store repair-run-lifecycles [--run-id <id>|--all] -> api.store_repair_run_lifecycles -> features.telemetry.reconcile_run_lifecycles`
- Monitor:
  `cli monitor snapshot [--workspace <path>] [--no-refresh-cloud] [--json] -> api.build_monitor_snapshot -> numereng_viz.build_monitor_snapshot`
  `cli remote list|bootstrap-viz|doctor|repo sync|experiment sync|experiment pull|experiment launch|experiment status|experiment maintain|experiment stop|config push|run train -> api.remote_* -> features.remote_ops.*`
- Dataset tooling command:
  `uv run numereng dataset-tools build-downsampled-full --data-version v5.2`
- Cloud AWS:
  `cli cloud aws * -> api.cloud_aws_* -> features.cloud.aws.*`
- Cloud Modal:
  `cli cloud modal * -> api.cloud_modal_* -> features.cloud.modal.*`
- Numerai forum scrape:
  `cli numerai forum scrape -> api.scrape_numerai_forum -> platform.scrape_forum_posts`
  (writes `posts/YYYY/MM/*.md` plus `INDEX.md` files at archive/year/month levels).
  Viz docs API surfaces a "Forum Archive" section in `/api/docs/numerai/tree` with year -> month nodes.
- Numerai docs browsing reads the repo-local `docs/numerai/` mirror when present; missing local mirrors surface an explicit `uv run numereng docs sync numerai` hint instead of a generic missing README state.
- Viz backend:
  `/api/* -> features.viz.routes/services/store_adapter`

Telemetry launch-source precedence:
- Source binding is context-based via `bind_launch_metadata(...)` / `get_launch_metadata()`.
- CLI run binds `cli.run.train`; CLI experiment train binds `cli.experiment.train`.
- API defaults bind only when unbound (`api.run.train`, `api.experiment.train`).
- Public workflow entrypoint defaults bind when unbound (`api.pipeline.train`).
- HPO fallback default source for trials: `api.hpo.create` (only when unbound).
- Modal runtime fallback default source: `cloud.modal.runtime` (only when unbound).

Local run telemetry write path:
- `features.training.run_training` reads launch metadata.
- Public local train entrypoints must bind launch metadata before calling the training pipeline.
- Training bootstrap requires launch metadata and a successful lifecycle session (`begin_local_training_session`) before dataset/model work starts.
- Bootstrap writes canonical current-truth surfaces:
  - `run_jobs`
  - `run_attempts`
  - `logical_runs`
  - `run_lifecycles`
  - `runs/<run_id>/runtime.json`
- During execution, training also appends:
  - `run_job_events`
  - `run_job_logs`
  - `run_job_samples`
- Status lifecycle: `queued -> starting -> running -> completed|failed|canceled|stale`.
- `run_lifecycles` is the canonical sqlite read model for current run state; `runtime.json` is the canonical filesystem snapshot for current run state.
- `run.json -> execution` is the canonical durable record of where a materialized run executed (`local`, `remote_host`, or `cloud`) and on which backend/provider.
- Both surfaces also carry canonical backend-owned progress fields:
  `progress_percent`, `progress_label`, `progress_current`, `progress_total`.
- `run_job_events` / `run_job_logs` / `run_job_samples` remain append-only history, not the source of current truth.
- Cooperative cancel is by `run_id` and sets `cancel_requested` in lifecycle/job rows; training exits through canonical `canceled` finalization at safe checkpoints.
- Active lifecycle reads may auto-reconcile orphaned local runs to `canceled` or `stale`.
- Lifecycle bootstrap is mandatory for local public training; later telemetry event/log/sample updates remain fail-open.

Experiment scoping behavior:
- Preferred: pass `experiment_id` explicitly (`run train --experiment-id ...` or experiment train flow).
- Fallback: telemetry infers experiment from config path under `.numereng/experiments/<id>/configs/*`.
- Archived experiments live under `.numereng/experiments/_archive/<id>/configs/*`; direct reads are archive-aware, but archived experiments are read-only.

Viz/frontend topology:
- Frontend routes: `/docs`, `/docs/numerai`, `/notes`, `/experiments`, `/experiments/[id]`.
- Viz backend OpenAPI docs are intentionally namespaced under `/api/docs`, with the schema at `/api/openapi.json`, so the frontend docs pages keep exclusive ownership of `/docs`.
- Notes routing caveat:
  - note-relative markdown links are the safe navigation primitive inside `.numereng/notes/__RESEARCH_MEMORY__/`
  - use explicit app routes like `/experiments/<id>` or `/experiments/<id>/runs/<run_id>` for experiment navigation from notes
- Viz backend launcher shim is `viz/api.py`; the actual backend package is `viz/api/numereng_viz`.
- No standalone frontend routes for `/run-ops` or `/configs`.
- Viz frontend is SSR-first again. Server-side route loads read the backend from `http://127.0.0.1:8502/api` by default and may be overridden with `VIZ_API_BASE`; browser reads still use `/api`.
- The root layout is intentionally local-fast: it loads only the local experiment list for the sidebar plus system capabilities. Remote-aware mission-control data no longer lives in the global shell.
- `/experiments` is the mission-control dashboard. It SSR-loads `/api/experiments/overview?include_remote=false`, keeps a page-local snapshot, then refreshes `/api/experiments/overview?include_remote=true` after hydration and every 10 seconds while the page is visible. The left rail lists local experiments from the root shell; the right pane shows live experiments plus recent terminal activity and attention states.
- Experiment and run detail routes keep the existing local path shape and add optional `source_kind` + `source_id` query params. By default the local viz backend now resolves canonical local run storage first, keeps a narrow read-only compatibility fallback for older pulled-cache artifacts under `.numereng/cache/remote_ops/pulls`, then falls back to SSH when there is exactly one eligible remote source.
- Mission control also exposes remote source health from `overview.sources`; enabled SSH remotes can appear as `live`, `cached`, or `unavailable`, and carry persisted bootstrap metadata (`bootstrap_status`, `last_bootstrap_at`, `last_bootstrap_error`) from the local viz bootstrap step.
- Mission control live cards render one canonical progress instrument per live experiment, chosen from the freshest active `primary` run (`updated_at desc`, then `exact > estimated > indeterminate`, then `run_id`). The card keeps a single progress bar plus adjacent percent instead of stacked aggregate/per-run rails.
- Experiment page contains Analysis + Progress + Run Ops tabs; all launch/control remains monitor-only and CLI/API-driven.
- The Progress tab is the only experiment-detail UI surface that live-polls per-run lifecycle state. It reads `/api/run-jobs?experiment_id=<id>` plus per-run `/api/runs/{run_id}/lifecycle`, keeps a page-local lifecycle cache, and renders backend-owned progress bars without inferring percent in the browser.
- Progress display labels map canonical lifecycle state to UI-only labels:
  `NOT_STARTED`, `QUEUED`, `TRAINING`, `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `STALE`.
- Run launch/control is CLI/API-only.
- Monitor snapshots are now a public read-only package contract:
  - Python API: `numereng.api.build_monitor_snapshot`
  - CLI: `numereng monitor snapshot [--workspace <path>] --json`
- Snapshot monitoring is store-based:
  - materialized local/remote/cloud runs read durable backend/provider provenance from `runs/<run_id>/run.json -> execution`
  - local live progress comes from active `run_lifecycles`
  - SageMaker/Batch jobs that are not yet materialized into `runs/<run_id>` are refreshed from `cloud_jobs`
- SSH remotes are polled by running `numereng monitor snapshot --json` on the remote numereng repo/store through configured remote profiles; the poller builds either a POSIX `cd ... && ...` command or an explicit `powershell -NoProfile -Command "Set-Location ...; ..."` command based on the profile shell.
- Mission control now canonicalizes by `experiment_id`: when an experiment exists both locally and on one SSH remote, the overview keeps one local-primary row and annotates it with remote freshness overlay metadata instead of rendering duplicate cards.
- `numereng remote bootstrap-viz` is the canonical federated viz bootstrap flow. It loads all enabled remote profiles, runs repo sync in `auto` mode, then runs `remote doctor`, and persists per-target `ready|degraded` state under `<store_root>/remote_ops/bootstrap/viz.json`.
- `just viz` is the canonical dashboard launcher for the repo-root workflow.
- `remote repo sync` mirrors the local git-visible working tree only; it never mirrors the full `.numereng` store and never syncs `src/numereng/platform/remotes/profiles/*.yaml`
- `remote experiment sync` mirrors only experiment authoring files under `.numereng/experiments/<id>/` and never overwrites a live remote `experiment.json`; remote manifests are runtime-owned after creation.
- `remote experiment pull` is the canonical remote result sync flow for remote-trained experiments. It materializes full `FINISHED` remote run directories into `.numereng/runs/<run_id>`, reconciles `.numereng/experiments/<id>/experiment.json` locally using remote runtime truth, skips already-materialized valid runs idempotently on rerun, and leaves active/incomplete remote runs to SSH fallback plus overview freshness overlay.
- `remote experiment launch|status|maintain|stop` are the canonical source-owned remote experiment window controls; detached execution is expressed as `experiment run-plan` plus a durable state file under `.numereng/remote_ops/experiment_run_plan/`.
- Local experiment and run detail reads are now strict local-first hot paths: if the local experiment or run artifacts exist, the viz API returns them directly without probing remote snapshot overlays. SSH detail fallback only happens for explicit remote source requests or genuine local misses.
- `remote run train` optionally repo-syncs first, syncs the experiment bundle when the config lives under `.numereng/experiments/<id>/configs/*`, and otherwise pushes the config into `repo_root/.numereng/tmp/remote-configs/*` before detached launch
- Live-run payloads now carry `progress_mode`:
  - `exact` for local lifecycle-backed percentages
  - `estimated` for cloud runs with coarse phase/status mapping
  - `indeterminate` when no honest percent can be derived
- Cloud progress is deliberately coarse and phase-based in mission control v1:
  `queued/submitted/pending/created/validating=0`, `starting=8`, `downloading=22`,
  `training=68`, `uploading=92`, `stopping=96`, `completed=100`.
  Failed/canceled/stale cloud rows preserve `last_progress_percent` when present.
- `experiment pack` writes `EXPERIMENT.pack.md` beside `EXPERIMENT.md`; it embeds the current experiment narrative plus a dashboard-aligned scalar run-metrics table only (no per-era/time-series payloads).
- Backend still exposes read-only catalog/query endpoints (`/experiments/overview`, `/configs`, `/experiments/{id}/configs`, `/run-jobs*`).
- Normal experiment/config catalogs exclude archived experiments; direct experiment detail/doc/config reads remain available by ID.
- Viz metric payloads are canonical-only: `corr_*`, `fnc_*`, `mmc_*`, `bmc_*`, `bmc_last_200_eras_mean`, `cwmm_*`, feature exposure metrics, `max_drawdown`, and `mmc_coverage_ratio_rows`.
- Viz also exposes payout-target aliases `corr_payout_mean` and `mmc_payout_mean`; `corr_payout_mean` comes from `corr_ender20`, while `mmc_mean` / `mmc_payout_mean` now normalize to the payout-backed MMC surface with legacy `mmc_ender20` fallback for older runs.
- Run-detail scoring charts canonicalize legacy contribution keys onto the payout-backed surface: `bmc`, `mmc`, and `corr_delta_vs_baseline`. Legacy `bmc_ender20` / `mmc_ender20` / `corr_delta_vs_baseline_ender20` map into those slots, while `baseline_corr_*` and native contribution duplicates are hidden from the main chart surface.
- Run detail stays on the dedicated `/experiments/[id]/runs/[run_id]` route but is local-first and lighter-weight: initial paint reads `/api/runs/{run_id}/bundle?sections=manifest,metrics,scoring_dashboard`, then diagnostics/artifacts/timeline sections lazy-load only their own bundle sections on first open.
- Training persists `post_fold` artifacts under `artifacts/scoring/`; `run score` and `experiment score-round` materialize deferred scoring artifacts there as well, with `artifacts/scoring/manifest.json` as the run-local entrypoint.
- There is one official persisted-run scorer. It supports stage selection: `all`, `run_metric_series`, `post_fold`, `post_training_core`, `post_training_full`.
- The canonical scorer preserves existing public contracts but now executes core per-era metric math through NumPy/Numba kernels instead of pandas `groupby.apply` callbacks.
- Viz prefers persisted scoring artifacts, and historical runs can be backfilled via `numereng store materialize-viz-artifacts --kind scoring-artifacts ...`; `--kind per-era-corr` is a deprecated alias to the same backfill path. Explicit `run_id` scope hard-fails with `store_run_not_found:<id>` when absent, while `experiment_id` scope skips unreadable unrelated runs. Legacy scoring fallback is read-only and does not write artifacts on request.

Critical invariants:
- `baseline build` requires at least two persisted run IDs, a safe baseline name, and a `default_target` shared by every source run.
- Successful `--promote-active` baseline builds refresh the shared active benchmark artifacts consumed by payout-target scoring fallback paths.
- Submission source XOR: exactly one of `run_id` or `predictions_path`.
- Classic submissions use `numerai_tools.submissions.validate_submission_numerai` against the current classic `live.parquet` id universe unless `allow_non_live_artifact=true`.
- Neutralization source XOR: exactly one of `run_id` or `predictions_path`.
- `run_id` deterministic hash-derived 12-char prefix.
- Training enforces single-writer execution per deterministic `run_id` via `runs/<run_id>/.train.lock`; concurrent starts hard-fail.
- Training requires a fresh deterministic run directory (no pre-existing artifacts besides `.train.lock`), so reruns after partial/failed attempts require store reset.
- Training and HPO config paths must be `.json`; unknown keys hard-fail.
- HPO v2 is a clean break: study configs must provide `study_id`, `objective`, `search_space`, `sampler`, and `stopping`; legacy flat HPO JSON is not supported.
- HPO search spaces are explicit-only; numereng no longer infers them from base model params.
- HPO studies resume by `study_id` using `.numereng/hpo/<study_id>/optuna_journal.log` or `.numereng/experiments/<experiment_id>/hpo/<study_id>/optuna_journal.log`.
- `sampler.kind=random` only allows `kind` and `seed`; TPE-only fields are invalid.
- `stopping.max_trials` and `stopping.max_completed_trials` are total-study caps across resumes; `stopping.timeout_seconds` is per `hpo create` invocation.
- HPO defaults to `bmc_last_200_eras.mean`.
- Legacy `post_fold_champion_objective` reads `post_fold_snapshots.parquet` first using `corr_ender20_fold_mean -> corr_native_fold_mean` and `bmc_fold_mean -> bmc_ender20_fold_mean` fallback, then falls back to `results.json`.
- Duplicate deterministic HPO params reuse a completed study trial or a finished deterministic run when its artifacts are intact; nonterminal pre-existing run dirs fail loudly and are never auto-reset.
- Legacy `data.loading` is removed from the training schema. Config loading hard-fails if it is present, but run hashing strips that removed subtree so historical configs keep the same deterministic run identity.
- `model.device` is the canonical explicit training-device contract and is allowed only: `cpu|cuda`.
- `model.device` is valid only for `LGBMRegressor`; if `model.params.device_type` is also set it must exactly match.
- Windows LightGBM GPU/OpenCL runs should use `model.params.device_type=gpu` without top-level `model.device`; `model.device=gpu` is invalid.
- Training profile allowed only: `simple|purged_walk_forward|full_history_refit`.
- Active-target rows with null or non-finite labels are treated as unlabeled for that target: training drops them before fit, target-aware validation/scoring excludes them, and folds with zero labeled rows fail with `training_target_rows_all_unlabeled:split=<split>:target=<target_col>[:fold=<n>]`.
- Legacy run flags `--method` / `--method-overrides-json` are hard-fail.
- Model fitting/prediction persistence completes first. Training writes `post_fold` artifacts during CV, then applies the resolved `training.post_training_scoring` policy: `none` leaves scoring deferred, `core` refreshes `post_training_core`, `full` refreshes inclusive `post_training_full`, and `round_core|round_full` defer single-run scoring for later experiment round batch materialization.
- Scoring persists `results.json`, `metrics.json`, `score_provenance.json`, and the canonical `artifacts/scoring/` bundle. The bundle writes `run_metric_series.parquet` plus staged parquet artifacts: `post_fold_per_era`, `post_fold_snapshots`, `post_training_core_summary`, and `post_training_full_summary` when FNC diagnostics are materialized. Stage-limited `post_training_core` and `post_training_full` rescoring also persist `run_metric_series.parquet` so viz retains the standard per-era and cumulative charts after summary refreshes. Historical runs may still have legacy `post_training_summary` / `post_training_features_summary` files, which remain readable.
- Deferred or failed post-training scoring still leaves `results.json` and `metrics.json` on disk with `training.scoring` status metadata; `score_provenance.json` and staged scoring artifacts appear once scoring materializes successfully.
- Training-time and persisted-run scoring metadata derive `emitted_stage_files` and recorded artifact file lists from the persisted `artifacts/scoring/manifest.json`, so `results.json`, `run.json`, and `score_provenance.json` match the materialized bundle exactly.
- `post_training_core_summary` is the flattened scorecard stage: native/payout CORR summaries plus payout-backed `mmc`, `bmc`, `bmc_last_200_eras`, scalar `avg_corr_with_benchmark`, and `corr_delta_vs_baseline` summary stats when those inputs are available.
- When the requested stage is only `post_training_core`, provenance/metadata should report `post_training_full` as `not_requested` rather than `feature_diagnostics_unavailable`; that omission switches to availability-based reasons only when `all` or `post_training_full` was actually requested.
- `baseline_corr` is not a persisted run metric. The scorer derives payout-target `corr_delta_vs_baseline` from the shared active-benchmark per-era CORR artifact when present, or from transient fallback computation for explicit path benchmarks.
- `corr`, `fnc`, and `mmc` delegate directly to `numerai_tools`; `cwmm` uses the official diagnostic semantics of Pearson correlation between the Numerai-transformed submission and the raw meta-model series.
- Training scoring does not emit payout estimate fields because Numereng does not implement an official expected-payout estimator from validation metrics.
- The experiment-page payout proxy chart is a research visualization only; it compares runs on the common payout-target plane (`corr_payout_mean`, `mmc_payout_mean`) so heterogeneous target runs are evaluated on the same Ender20-based basis.
- The experiment-page analysis panel now has three chart views: `BMC Ranking`, `Payout Proxy`, and `Target Analysis`. `Target Analysis` is frontend-only filtering over actual runs and compares native `corr_mean` vs payout-backed `mmc_mean` within one selected training target.
- Viz defaults rank/sort experiment views by `bmc_last_200_eras_mean`.
- Canonical post-training diagnostics are FNC-only: scoring neutralizes against `fncv3_features`, persists `fnc` summaries, and no longer emits feature-exposure summaries as part of canonical scoring artifacts or viz-normalized run metrics.
- Post-training scoring is best-effort: scoring exceptions leave the training run `FINISHED` and record failure state under `training.scoring` plus `metrics.json`.
- `features.scoring.run_service.score_run` can recompute the same staged scoring bundle for an already-persisted run and refresh `results.json`/`metrics.json`/`run.json` plus store index rows, including `artifacts/scoring/manifest.json`. Stage-selective rescoring overwrites only the requested canonical stage artifacts, leaves non-selected existing stage files in place, and rewrites both `score_provenance.json -> artifacts` and `training.scoring.emitted_stage_files` from the persisted scoring manifest so recorded charts/stage files match the materialized bundle.
- Canonical FNC neutralizes against dataset feature set `fncv3_features`, independent of the run training feature set, then correlates against the scoring target being evaluated (`fnc` for the native target, `fnc_<alias>` for extra scoring targets).
- `score_provenance.json` records `policy.fnc_feature_set=fncv3_features`, `policy.fnc_target_policy=scoring_target`, `benchmark_min_overlap_ratio=0.0`, join row/era counts, benchmark/meta missing-row/era counts, whether meta-dependent metrics were emitted, and the persisted scoring-artifact manifest summary.
- `experiment score-round` resolves only eligible experiment runs for each round config stem: run status must be `FINISHED` and persisted predictions must exist. If multiple eligible runs share the same config stem, the newest run wins.
- Benchmark post-run scoring joins require strict era alignment, but `bmc` / `bmc_last_200_eras` are computed on the maximum available overlapping benchmark window whenever any overlap exists.
- Meta-model post-run scoring joins require strict era alignment, but `mmc` / `cwmm` are computed on the maximum available overlapping meta-model window whenever any overlap exists.
- Training writes run-local live stage/status logs to `runs/<run_id>/run.log` and records that path in `run.json -> artifacts.log`.
- Dataset scope allowed only: `train_only|train_plus_validation` (default `train_only`).
- `data.dataset_variant` is required and allowed only: `non_downsampled|downsampled`.
- Dataset defaults resolve under `.numereng/datasets/<data_version>/`.
- Canonical non-downsampled storage uses split sources under `.numereng/datasets/<data_version>/`:
  `train.parquet`, `validation.parquet`, plus split benchmark files.
- Stored derived downsampled artifacts are:
  `downsampled_full.parquet`, `downsampled_full_benchmark_models.parquet`.
- `dataset-tools build-downsampled-full` defaults to fixed era filtering (keep every 4th era, offset 0), with optional `--downsample-eras-step` and `--downsample-eras-offset`.
- In `purged_walk_forward`, walk-forward defaults are fixed: window `156`, embargo `8` for `20d` and `16` for `60d`.
- `data.target_horizon` is `20d|60d` when provided and takes precedence over target-name inference.
- If `target_horizon` is omitted, horizon is inferred from `target_col`; ambiguous names hard-fail.
- Benchmark model parquet data is used for scoring metrics (BMC/correlation-to-benchmark), not as training features.
- Canonical `model.x_groups` / `model.data_needed` are features-only by default; `era` and `id` are never auto-included and are rejected as input groups.
- `model.x_groups` hard-fails on benchmark aliases (`benchmark`, `benchmarks`, `benchmark_models`) with `training_model_x_groups_benchmark_not_supported`.
- In `train_plus_validation` mode, `data_type=validation` filtering is applied only to validation sources (never to `train.parquet`).
- Training treats rows with null or non-finite values in the active `target_col` as unlabeled for that target, so validation prediction/scoring artifacts only include labeled rows for that target and train/validation folds fail explicitly when filtering would leave zero labeled rows.
- Purged walk-forward CV requires at least `chunk_size + 1` unique eras; otherwise training fails with an explicit configuration error.
- `full_history_refit` is final-fit only for training; it skips `post_fold` because there is no CV artifact surface, and post-training scoring remains policy-driven in the same way as other profiles.
- `training.resources.max_threads_per_worker` accepts integer >= 1 or `"default"`; `"default"` (and omitted/`null` for backward compatibility) resolves to `max(1, floor(available_cpus / parallel_folds))`.
- Training never performs row-level sampling; dataset-size reduction must be done via dataset-level downsampling.
- Training command requires successful pre-finalization `index_run`; post-finish index refresh is best-effort.
- Experiment train defaults `output_dir` to `store_root`; when provided it must equal `store_root`.
- `experiment archive` moves only experiment-local files; run directories remain canonical under `.numereng/runs/*`.
- Archived experiments are operationally frozen: `experiment train` and `experiment promote` hard-fail until unarchived.
- Ensemble supports only `rank_avg` and >=2 deduped run IDs.
- Cloud AWS submit backend allowed only `sagemaker|batch`; `--spot` and `--on-demand` are mutually exclusive.
- `cloud aws train pull` is download-only and writes managed artifacts under `.numereng/cache/cloud/aws/runs/<run_id>/pull`.
- `cloud aws train extract` is the separate step that unpacks pulled tarballs into `.numereng/runs/*` and indexes runs.
- `cloud aws train extract` only promotes `runs/<run_id>/*` members from archives; non-`runs/*` members are skipped with warnings.
- Managed archive extraction hard-fails on unsafe tar members (absolute/traversal paths, links, invalid run ids) and on run-dir hash conflicts.
- Managed extraction indexes only extracted run IDs (no fallback indexing of outer cloud run id).
- If managed extraction sees an existing `runs/<run_id>` with the same deterministic run hash, it skips moving files but still refreshes that run's durable `execution` provenance from managed state before re-indexing it.
- SageMaker managed entrypoint deletes `numereng.db`, `numereng.db-shm`, and `numereng.db-wal` from output artifacts before packaging.
- Materialized cloud and remote-host runs persist durable provenance in `runs/<run_id>/run.json -> execution`; transient cloud state/pull staging under `cache/cloud` is disposable after extraction.
- `.numereng/tmp/*` is store-owned scratch space; `store doctor --fix-strays` may prune `tmp/remote-configs/*.json` files older than 30 days when they are not referenced by active run lifecycles.
- Managed AWS `--state-path` defaults under `<store_root>/cache/cloud/aws/...`; explicit overrides must resolve under `<store_root>/cache/cloud/**/*.json` or legacy `<store_root>/cloud/*.json` during migration.
- Managed AWS submit trims `NUMERENG_RUN_EXECUTION_JSON` to the provider/runtime fields needed inside the container so long descriptive run IDs and canonical state paths stay under SageMaker's env-value limit; full provenance is still recovered from managed state during pull/extract.
- Managed AWS/EC2 cloud `runtime_profile` selects packaging only (`standard|lgbm-cuda`) and never overrides the training config device.
- SageMaker submit may omit `--image-uri`; numereng resolves the checked-in default alias for the selected `runtime_profile` and persists both the resolved `image_uri` and tag digest into managed state/cloud metadata.
- SageMaker CUDA runs require all three: config resolves to `model.device=cuda`, `runtime_profile=lgbm-cuda`, and a GPU instance type (`ml.g*` or `ml.p*`).
- EC2 CUDA runtime install requires a GPU instance state and `cloud ec2 install --runtime-profile lgbm-cuda`.
- CUDA training is fail-fast; numereng does not silently fall back from `cuda` to CPU.
- EC2 and Modal `--state-path` follow the same cache-first rule: default under `<store_root>/cache/cloud/...`, explicit override under `<store_root>/cache/cloud/**/*.json`, legacy `<store_root>/cloud/*.json` accepted for compatibility only.
- EC2 `pull` and `s3 cp` prefix downloads skip unsafe relative keys and report them in `skipped_unsafe_keys`.
- Cloud Modal deploy requires ECR URI `<registry>/<repository>:<tag>`.
- Cloud state file flags (`--state-path`) must decode to valid typed JSON state objects.

CLI families:
- `numereng run train|score|submit`
- `numereng experiment create|list|details|archive|unarchive|train|score-round|promote|report|pack`
- `numereng research status|run`
- `numereng hpo create|list|details|trials`
- `numereng ensemble build|select|list|details`
- `numereng neutralize apply`
- `numereng dataset-tools build-downsampled-full`
- `numereng store init|index|rebuild|doctor|materialize-viz-artifacts`
- `numereng cloud ec2 ...`
- `numereng cloud aws image|train ...`
- `numereng cloud modal deploy|data sync|train ...`
- `numereng numerai datasets (list|download) | models | round | forum scrape`

Experiment scaffolding:
- `experiment create` requires experiment IDs in `YYYY-MM-DD_slug` format.
- `experiment create` now scaffolds the deterministic experiment skeleton: `experiment.json`, a rich `EXPERIMENT.md`, `configs/`, a header-only `run_plan.csv`, and `run_scripts/launch_all.py|.sh|.ps1`.
- Scripted sweeps should treat the generated launchers as the canonical entrypoint and keep per-run training at `--post-training-scoring none`, then batch-score rounds with `experiment score-round`.

Verification anchors:
- Install `just` if you want to use the wrapper commands below.
- Format: `uv run ruff format --check .`
- Lint: `uv run ruff check .`
- Types: `uv run ty check`
- Fast: `just test`
- Full: `just test-all`
- Build: `just build`
- Surface smoke: `tests/integration/test_smoke_structure.py`

Tooling notes:
- Canonical local toolchain: `uv` + Ruff + `ty` + `pytest`.
- Ruff remains minimal: formatter plus lint families `E`, `F`, `I`, `UP`.
- `ty` adoption is intentionally staged through `[tool.ty.src].include` in `pyproject.toml`; do not widen the enforced surface casually.

When changing flows/contracts:
1) Preserve API/CLI compatibility unless intentionally versioned.
2) Keep dependency direction.
3) Keep boundary error translation to `PackageError`.
4) Update `docs/llms.txt` and `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` in same PR.
